evals.prompt: you analyzed my tennis video last week — now make me a training plan. I can practice twice a week, 90 minutes, I have a hitting partner and a wall at my gym. Name's Yi.
evals.expected_output: Reads latest report from ~/.tennis-analysis/players/yi/, interviews for remaining constraints (injuries, goal), builds 8-12 week periodized plan.json, renders training_plan.md + .html with phases, weekly tables, exit criteria, re-assessment loop.

evals.prompt: I'm about a 3.0 player, no video analysis yet. my serve is terrible and I get tired running around. give me a realistic practice schedule for 3 months, 3x a week
evals.expected_output: Fallback mode: offers video analysis first; on decline interviews self-description, builds plan flagged as self-description-based, realistic expectations per progression table.

evals.prompt: my 10-week plan is done, i did most of it. what now?
evals.expected_output: Directs to re-assessment: record new video, re-run tennis-video-analysis, compare radar/trend, then build next macrocycle from the new report's gates.
